pattern: Tally the available letters once, then spend them against the demand; the note is buildable exactly when no letter is requested more often than it is stocked.
hints: The order of letters never matters here — only how many of each you have versus how many you need.
Count the frequency of every letter in the source, then walk the requested letters subtracting from those counts.
If any letter's running count drops below zero (you needed more than were available), the answer is false; otherwise it is true.

edge_inputs.description: right letters but one is short a copy
source has extra letters but not the needed one
note is a single letter present many times
note longer than source can never succeed

stress.description: random note and a larger random source over a small alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:6]
    note = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(max(1, size // 4)))
    mag = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(max(1, size)))
    return [note, mag]
stress.note: small alphabet makes feasibility a coin-flip so both outcomes appear
